name: RTD Consumer
description: Convenience-focused consumer who prefers ready-to-drink beverages
context: RTD Consumer values convenience, portability, and simplicity above all. They want single-serve, pre-mixed, grab-and-go options that require no preparation, equipment, or expertise. They're often younger, mobile, and consumption happens on-the-go, at outdoor events, or in casual social settings. They prioritize ease over craft, consistency over customization, and portability over prestige. They're the fastest-growing alcohol segment, driven by hard seltzers, canned cocktails, and premixed spirits.

psychographics.decisionMakingStyle: Convenience-driven and occasion-based. Quick decisions. Prioritizes format over brand. Loyal to convenience, not necessarily to specific products.

alwaysAsks: Is this easy to grab and go?
Does this taste good cold from the can?
evaluationCriteria.reactsToEarlyIdeas: Evaluates through convenience and lifestyle fit lens. Wants portability, flavor variety, and social acceptability.
evaluationCriteria.scoringRubric: Scores on convenience, portability, flavor quality, variety, social fit, and price-per-occasion value.

exampleQuotes: I just want to grab a drink and go
Who wants to carry a whole bar setup to the beach?
These canned cocktails are perfect for hiking
I don't know how to make fancy drinks and I don't care
Give me something cold, easy, and tasty
keyInsights: RTD consumers are driving category growth—hard seltzers, canned cocktails, premixed spirits
They're format-loyal more than brand-loyal—convenience is the primary driver
Occasion-based consumption: outdoors, travel, casual gatherings, poolside
They're younger and more diverse than traditional spirits consumers
Flavor variety and innovation matter—they experiment within the RTD format
Quality has improved dramatically—premium RTDs can command higher prices
Marketing should emphasize lifestyle fit and occasion suitability, not just flavor
